claim: For ChatGPT's consumer base, improvements to model intelligence don't significantly drive usage growth; speed and ease of access matter more than reasoning
actionable: Consumer AI products should optimize for speed and distribution over reasoning depth; reasoning models (o1) are niche even for power users
source_title: The Big Lie About Smarter AI Models

claim: Once models reach PhD-level intelligence, incremental improvements yield marginal value for most use cases; faster inference and better integrations drive adoption
actionable: Stop expecting each model release to unlock new use cases; focus instead on distribution, integrations (Gmail, Calendar), and latency
source_title: The Big Lie About Smarter AI Models

claim: OpenAI faces internal tension between research division (pushing model capability) and product division (demanding speed and integrations)
actionable: Organizational misalignment on research vs. product is a risk; leaders should clarify whether their goal is best-in-class models or best-in-class products
source_title: The Big Lie About Smarter AI Models

claim: OpenAI has shifted compute away from research toward meeting production demand (Sora image generation viral usage); sacrificing future capability for present demand
actionable: Frontier labs face capacity constraints; expect periods where capability advancement pauses to maintain service reliability
source_title: The Big Lie About Smarter AI Models

claim: Google's competitive advantage lies in integrations (Gmail, Calendar, Drive); Gemini appearing everywhere is more valuable than being faster
actionable: Build or partner for distribution; model capability alone does not win consumer markets; ubiquity does
source_title: The Big Lie About Smarter AI Models

claim: Speed is undervalued in benchmarking; fast correct answers beat slow perfect ones for consumer search and chat applications
actionable: Measure LLM quality by speed + accuracy trade-offs, not accuracy alone; latency directly impacts user experience and adoption
source_title: The Big Lie About Smarter AI Models
